Traditionally, Gymnogramma elliptica has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Gymnogramma elliptica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Gymnogramma elliptica is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in combination with other herbs to treat various health conditions.
